Clicking outside or selecting a tool closes the menu. The toolbar should no longer overflow on narrow windows.

content.text: The dropdown menu is appearing behind the SVG canvas due to z-index stacking context. Let me check the canvas/toolbar layout to understand the stacking.

The toolbar wrapper in App has `z-index: 10` but lacks `position: relative`, so the z-index doesn't create a stacking context. The dropdown's `z-index: 100` is scoped inside `.dropdown` which has `position: relative`, but it needs the parent toolbar to also be positioned to layer above the canvas.

content.content: The file C:\Users\johnm\modular apartment planner\src\app\App.module.css has been updated successfully.

content.text: The toolbar wrapper needed `position: relative` for its `z-index: 10` to actually create a stacking context above the canvas. The dropdown menu should now render on top.

message.content: it still not showing, i think i was hidden by something

content.text: The `overflow-x: auto` on `.toolbar` (line 10) is clipping the dropdown menu since it overflows vertically. That's the problem — `overflow-x: auto` also implicitly sets `overflow-y` to `auto`/`hidden`, which clips absolutely positioned children.

content.content: The file C:\Users\johnm\modular apartment planner\src\ui\Toolbar.module.css has been updated successfully.

content.text: Removed `overflow-x: auto` from `.toolbar`. That property was clipping the dropdown menu because setting `overflow-x` to anything other than `visible` also forces `overflow-y` to clip, hiding the absolutely-positioned dropdown. With the tools group now a compact dropdown, the overflow scrolling shouldn't be needed anyway.
